items.title: FCA consults on guidance on UK’s future crypto regime
items.summary: FCA consults on guidance defining regulated cryptoasset activities under the UK's forthcoming crypto regime effective October 2027.
items.why_it_matters: Every UK crypto firm must assess licensing scope now; this guidance defines which activities trigger authorisation requirements.

items.title: Piero Cipollone: Sparking the transformation of finance: tokenisation and the role of central banks
items.summary: ECB board member Cipollone outlines central bank's role in tokenisation and the transformation of financial infrastructure.
items.why_it_matters: Signals ECB's strategic stance on tokenised finance and wholesale CBDC, directly shaping tokenisation platform and DeFi firm strategy.

items.title: SEC Approves Exemptive Order and Proposed Rule Change to Permit Customer Cross-Margining in the U.S. Treasury Market
items.summary: SEC approves cross-margining of cash US Treasury and Treasury futures positions at registered clearing agencies.
items.why_it_matters: Hedge funds and prop shops trading US Treasuries gain meaningful margin efficiency; reshapes collateral and liquidity management immediately.

items.title: Artificial Intelligence Consortium minutes – February 2026
items.summary: Bank of England AI Consortium February 2026 minutes covering AI capabilities, risks, and deployment in UK financial services.
items.why_it_matters: AI-native fintechs and model-driven firms should track BoE's emerging expectations on AI governance and risk in UK finance.

items.title: FCA sets out vision for open finance to empower consumers and businesses
items.summary: FCA publishes open finance vision enabling consumers and businesses to securely share financial data with providers.
items.why_it_matters: Fintechs and data-driven lenders should assess open finance roadmap now as it shapes future product and API strategy.

items.title: FCA fines Dinosaur Merchant Bank Limited for market abuse surveillance failures
items.summary: FCA fines Dinosaur Merchant Bank £338,000 for failing to maintain adequate market abuse surveillance on CFD business.
items.why_it_matters: CFD issuers, prop shops, and crypto derivatives platforms must review surveillance systems given FCA's clear enforcement posture.

items.title: FCA sets out next phase of smarter, more effective regulation
items.summary: FCA announces AI-powered authorisation tools, generative AI for regulatory modernisation, and data-driven supervision as part of 2026/27 plan.
items.why_it_matters: AI-native fintechs and applicants face a transformed, faster FCA authorisation process with greater AI-driven risk screening.

items.title: ESAs spring risk update highlights geopolitical pressures and rising private finance risks
items.summary: ESAs spring risk update flags elevated geopolitical risks, private finance vulnerabilities, and asset valuation pressures.
items.why_it_matters: Hedge funds and private credit funds should treat this as a supervisory signal that regulators are scrutinising their sector closely.

items.title: SEC Seeks Public Comment on the Consolidated Audit Trail and Other Audit Trails and Data Sources
items.summary: SEC issues concept release seeking public comment on a comprehensive review of the Consolidated Audit Trail.
items.why_it_matters: Prop shops and algo traders relying on CAT data or subject to CAT reporting should engage with this review now.

items.title: Year 2 Consumer Duty Board Reports: progress and what comes next
items.summary: FCA reviews Year 2 Consumer Duty board reports, setting out expectations for ongoing customer outcome monitoring.
items.why_it_matters: Fintechs with retail customers must demonstrate robust Consumer Duty governance or risk regulatory intervention.

items.title: ESMA launches a call for evidence on restricted subscription and private credit ratings
items.summary: ESMA calls for evidence on restricted subscription and private credit ratings practices in EU markets.
items.why_it_matters: Private credit funds and leveraged finance platforms operating in EU should monitor evolving rating transparency requirements.

items.title: ESMA releases reporting templates and instructions for the Active Account Requirement
items.summary: ESMA releases reporting templates for the Active Account Requirement under EMIR 3.0 for EU clearing obligations.
items.why_it_matters: EU-regulated derivatives firms and hedge funds must prepare systems for new Active Account Requirement reporting obligations.

items.title: ESMA clarifies expectations in the run-up to the launch of EU’s Consolidated Tapes
items.summary: ESMA clarifies compliance expectations for market participants ahead of the EU Consolidated Tape launch.
items.why_it_matters: Trading venues, systematic internalisers, and market-making fintechs need to align data reporting to Consolidated Tape requirements.

items.title: EU financial markets enter 2026 amid high-risk environment
items.summary: ESMA annual risk assessment finds EU financial markets entering 2026 in a high-risk environment with elevated volatility threats.
items.why_it_matters: Risk managers at hedge funds and crypto funds should use this as a supervisory benchmark when calibrating stress scenarios.

items.title: Clarus IV ICAV (CLONE) - Central Bank of Ireland Issues Warning on Unauthorised Firm
items.summary: Central Bank of Ireland warns against clone firm impersonating authorised ICAV Clarus IV, targeting investors.
items.why_it_matters: Crypto and alternative fund operators should monitor clone fraud warnings as reputational and investor protection risks are rising.

items.title: Pimco Global Wealth (Clone) - Central Bank of Ireland Issues Warning on Unauthorised Firm
items.summary: Central Bank of Ireland warns against unauthorised firm cloning Pimco Global Wealth to solicit investors.
items.why_it_matters: Clone firm activity targeting fund investors is escalating; compliance and brand protection teams at asset managers should act.

items.source_name: US Securities and Exchange Commission (8-K filings)
items.title: TERAWULF INC. — 8-K (Item 1.01: Entry into a Material Definitive Agreement, Item 8.01: Other Events, Item 9.01: Financial Statements and Exhibits)
items.summary: TeraWulf Inc. files 8-K disclosing entry into a material definitive agreement related to its Bitcoin mining operations.
items.why_it_matters: Crypto mining firms' capital structure moves affect hashrate economics and Bitcoin supply dynamics watched by crypto funds.

items.title: Meeting of 18-19 March 2026
items.summary: ECB Governing Council meeting minutes from March 2026 covering monetary policy decisions.
items.why_it_matters: Rate and balance sheet signals matter for macro hedge funds and liquidity managers but contain no frontier-specific rules.

items.title: Chairman Atkins Launches 'Material Matters' Podcast
items.summary: SEC Chairman Atkins launches a new podcast series to communicate agency policy and priorities.
items.why_it_matters: Could surface useful signals on SEC crypto and AI policy direction but is media content, not binding regulation.

items.title: SEC Small Business Advisory Committee to Explore Ways to Encourage More IPOs
items.summary: SEC advisory committee meeting to explore measures encouraging more companies to pursue IPOs.
items.why_it_matters: Relevant to fintech firms considering public listings but no immediate compliance obligations arise.

items.title: Minutes of the SONIA Stakeholder Advisory Group - 24 March 2026
items.summary: Minutes from SONIA Stakeholder Advisory Group meeting covering benchmark rate administration updates.
items.why_it_matters: Relevant for firms with SONIA-linked exposure but contains no new policy changes warranting urgent CRO attention.

items.title: CPMI-IOSCO assesses that the United Kingdom has implemented the Principles for financial market infrastructures for two FMI types, but recommends some improvements
items.summary: CPMI-IOSCO assesses UK financial market infrastructure compliance with PFMI principles, noting some improvement areas.
items.why_it_matters: Tokenisation platforms and crypto CCPs seeking FMI status should note gaps identified in UK infrastructure oversight framework.

items.title: Central Bank statement on High Court judgment
items.summary: Central Bank of Ireland High Court confirms fitness and probity prohibition against a senior executive.
items.why_it_matters: Reinforces individual accountability under fitness and probity regimes; relevant for Irish-domiciled fund and fintech senior managers.

items.title: Christine Lagarde: IMFC Statement
items.summary: ECB President Lagarde delivers standard IMFC statement on global economic outlook and ECB policy.
items.why_it_matters: Generic macro commentary with no direct regulatory or compliance implications for frontier finance firms.

items.title: Isabel Schnabel: Europe's successes and the path forward
items.summary: ECB board member Schnabel gives speech on Europe's economic achievements and future policy direction.
items.why_it_matters: Routine speech with no actionable regulatory content for fintechs, crypto funds, or prop shops.

items.title: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with Community Bankshares, Inc.
items.summary: Federal Reserve issues enforcement action against Community Bankshares, a small community bank.
items.why_it_matters: Legacy community bank action with no read-across to fintech, crypto, or modern financial firm operations.

items.title: Banknotes imagery advisory group minutes - August 2025
items.summary: Bank of England banknote imagery advisory group meeting minutes from August 2025.
items.why_it_matters: Entirely irrelevant to frontier finance; concerns physical banknote design decisions.

items.title: Banknote imagery advisory group minutes - October 2025
items.summary: Bank of England banknote imagery advisory group meeting minutes from October 2025.
items.why_it_matters: Entirely irrelevant to frontier finance; concerns physical banknote design decisions.

items.title: EBA E-mail alert 18 April, 2026
items.summary: EBA routine email alert digest for 18 April 2026 with no substantive content visible.
items.why_it_matters: Digest format with no extractable regulatory content; cannot be scored on substance.

items.title: EBA E-mail alert 17 April, 2026
items.summary: EBA routine email alert digest for 17 April 2026 with no substantive content visible.
items.why_it_matters: Digest format with no extractable regulatory content; cannot be scored on substance.

items.title: EBA E-mail alert 16 April, 2026
items.summary: EBA routine email alert digest for 16 April 2026 with no substantive content visible.
items.why_it_matters: Digest format with no extractable regulatory content; cannot be scored on substance.
